question: Are we too dependent on certain regions for revenue?

trace.text:
WITH
max_week_end_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(d.week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM data_867 d
),
time_window_cte AS (
  SELECT
    m.max_week_end_date AS window_end_date,
    DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AS window_start_date
  FROM max_week_end_date_cte m
),
region_agg_cte AS (
  SELECT
    d.campus_region,
    SUM(d.rytelo_total_mg) AS region_sales_52w
  FROM data_867 d
  CROSS JOIN time_window_cte tw
  WHERE d.week_end_date >= tw.window_start_date
    AND d.week_end_date <= tw.window_end_date
  GROUP BY d.campus_region
),
nation_agg_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(d.rytelo_total_mg) AS nation_sales_52w
  FROM data_867 d
  CROSS JOIN time_window_cte tw
  WHERE d.week_end_date >= tw.window_start_date
    AND d.week_end_date <= tw.window_end_date
),
contribution_cte AS (
  SELECT
    r.campus_region,
    r.region_sales_52w,
    n.nation_sales_52w,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY r.region_sales_52w DESC) AS region_rank,
    ROUND((r.region_sales_52w / NULLIF(n.nation_sales_52w, 0)) * 100, 0) AS region_contribution_52w_pct_int
  FROM region_agg_cte r
  CROSS JOIN nation_agg_cte n
),
concentration_summary_cte AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN c.region_rank = 1 THEN c.region_contribution_52w_pct_int ELSE 0 END) AS top_1_region_contribution_52w_pct_int,
    SUM(CASE WHEN c.region_rank <= 2 THEN c.region_contribution_52w_pct_int ELSE 0 END) AS top_2_regions_contribution_52w_pct_int,
    SUM(CASE WHEN c.region_rank <= 3 THEN c.region_contribution_52w_pct_int ELSE 0 END) AS top_3_regions_contribution_52w_pct_int
  FROM contribution_cte c
)
SELECT
  MAX(tw.window_start_date) AS window_start_date,
  MAX(tw.window_end_date) AS window_end_date,
  c.campus_region,
  c.region_sales_52w,
  c.nation_sales_52w,
  CONCAT(c.region_contribution_52w_pct_int, '%') AS `region_contribution_52w%`,
  c.region_rank,
  CONCAT(MAX(cs.top_1_region_contribution_52w_pct_int), '%') AS `top_1_region_contribution_52w%`,
  CONCAT(MAX(cs.top_2_regions_contribution_52w_pct_int), '%') AS `top_2_regions_contribution_52w%`,
  CONCAT(MAX(cs.top_3_regions_contribution_52w_pct_int), '%') AS `top_3_regions_contribution_52w%`
FROM contribution_cte c
CROSS JOIN time_window_cte tw
CROSS JOIN concentration_summary_cte cs
GROUP BY
  c.campus_region,
  c.region_sales_52w,
  c.nation_sales_52w,
  c.region_contribution_52w_pct_int,
  c.region_rank
ORDER BY c.region_sales_52w DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: How does our breadth look like?

trace.text:
WITH max_week_end_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM data_867
),
rolling_52w_window_bounds AS (
  SELECT
    max_week_end_date AS window_end_date_52w,
    DATE_SUB(max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 51 WEEK) AS window_start_date_52w
  FROM max_week_end_date_cte
)
SELECT
  MAX(w.window_start_date_52w) AS window_start_date_52w,
  MAX(w.window_end_date_52w) AS window_end_date_52w,
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN d.rytelo_total_mg > 0 THEN d.campus_id END) AS active_campus_accounts_52w,
  SUM(d.rytelo_total_mg) AS sales_mg_52w
FROM data_867 d
CROSS JOIN rolling_52w_window_bounds w
WHERE d.week_end_date >= w.window_start_date_52w
  AND d.week_end_date <= w.window_end_date_52w;

question: How are we doing in terms of adding new businesses?

trace.text:
WITH
max_week_end_date_cte AS (
  SELECT MAX(week_end_date) AS max_week_end_date
  FROM data_867
),
rolling_windows_cte AS (
  SELECT
    m.max_week_end_date AS r13w_end_week_end_date,
    DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 12 WEEK) AS r13w_start_week_end_date,
    DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 13 WEEK) AS p13w_end_week_end_date,
    DATE_SUB(m.max_week_end_date, INTERVAL 25 WEEK) AS p13w_start_week_end_date
  FROM max_week_end_date_cte m
),
campus_first_active_week_cte AS (
  SELECT
    campus_id,
    MIN(week_end_date) AS first_active_week_end_date
  FROM data_867
  WHERE rytelo_total_mg > 0
  GROUP BY campus_id
),
new_business_counts_cte AS (
  SELECT
    w.p13w_start_week_end_date,
    w.p13w_end_week_end_date,
    w.r13w_start_week_end_date,
    w.r13w_end_week_end_date,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
      WHEN c.first_active_week_end_date BETWEEN w.r13w_start_week_end_date AND w.r13w_end_week_end_date
      THEN c.campus_id
    END) AS new_business_accounts_r13w,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CASE
      WHEN c.first_active_week_end_date BETWEEN w.p13w_start_week_end_date AND w.p13w_end_week_end_date
      THEN c.campus_id
    END) AS new_business_accounts_p13w
  FROM rolling_windows_cte w
  CROSS JOIN campus_first_active_week_cte c
  GROUP BY
    w.p13w_start_week_end_date,
    w.p13w_end_week_end_date,
    w.r13w_start_week_end_date,
    w.r13w_end_week_end_date
),
growth_calc_cte AS (
  SELECT
    p13w_start_week_end_date,
    p13w_end_week_end_date,
    new_business_accounts_p13w,
    r13w_start_week_end_date,
    r13w_end_week_end_date,
    new_business_accounts_r13w,
    CONCAT(
      ROUND(
        CASE
          WHEN new_business_accounts_p13w = 0 AND new_business_accounts_r13w > 0 THEN 100
          WHEN new_business_accounts_p13w = 0 AND new_business_accounts_r13w = 0 THEN 0
          ELSE ((new_business_accounts_r13w - new_business_accounts_p13w) / NULLIF(new_business_accounts_p13w, 0)) * 100
        END,
        0
      ),
      '%'
    ) AS growth_new_business_r13w_vs_p13w_pct
  FROM new_business_counts_cte
)
SELECT
  p13w_start_week_end_date,
  p13w_end_week_end_date,
  new_business_accounts_p13w,
  r13w_start_week_end_date,
  r13w_end_week_end_date,
  new_business_accounts_r13w,
  growth_new_business_r13w_vs_p13w_pct
FROM growth_calc_cte;
